summary: Comprehensive testing of EtsyWatch full-stack app. 17/17 backend pytest tests passed. All key UI flows verified: landing page + pricing, signup, protected routes, dashboard CRUD, settings persistence, evidence empty state, header navigation, and logout.

minor.issue: allow_origins='*' combined with allow_credentials=True is invalid per CORS spec; browsers will reject credentialed requests. Currently no auth credentials needed but should be tightened for prod.

Backend lowercases email but frontend stores the email as typed in localStorage (SESSION_KEY). If a user types 'User@Example.com', session header 'X-User-Email: User@Example.com' won't match DB record 'user@example.com' and /auth/me would 401. Frontend should call setSession(email.toLowerCase()) or backend should match case-insensitively.
Deprecated FastAPI hooks; will be removed in future versions. Use lifespan context manager.
No rate limiting / captcha - anyone can spam-create users since there is no password.

issues: Logout is only an icon with no accessible label or data-testid; harder to test and not screen-reader friendly. Suggest adding data-testid='logout-btn' and aria-label='Logout'.

action_items: Add data-testid='logout-btn' and aria-label to the logout icon button in Header for testability
Lowercase email in setSession() in /app/frontend/src/lib/api.js (or normalize on backend) to avoid case-mismatch 401s
Tighten CORS for production (do not pair '*' with allow_credentials=True)
Optional: migrate FastAPI startup/shutdown to lifespan context manager
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 634 lines - acceptable now but watch growth; consider splitting scraping/email rendering into modules if expanded.

Evidence download endpoint fetches remote screenshot (Unsplash) synchronously on each request; should cache bytes locally or stream via httpx to avoid latency / outbound failure surfacing as empty file (no status check on response).
check_user_listings runs all listings sequentially per user inside a serial loop over users; for many users this could be slow. Acceptable for MVP.
title_similarity uses SequenceMatcher - reasonable for MVP; consider token-set ratio (rapidfuzz) for better robustness against word order.
No db indexes declared (e.g. on users.email, listings.user_email, evidence.listing_id) - fine for low volume.
test_credentials: No fixed test credentials needed. Auth uses any unique email; tests generate emails like test_a_<uuid>@example.com.
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up transient test users/listings inside the pytest run (uuid-suffixed emails so no collision).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_etsywatch.py covers auth, listings CRUD, free-plan limit (3), multi-tenancy, check-now, settings, evidence empty/404. To re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_etsywatch.py -v. Etsy scraping with the fake URL https://www.etsy.com/listing/1234567890/test currently succeeds (server returns content) so title may parse as something non-empty. The scheduler starts at boot ('EtsyWatch scheduler started' in backend logs). Resend is in MOCKED mode (logs only). For UI test the only minor friction is the logout icon lacks data-testid - use last button in header element instead.
rca of the issue: No critical RCAs - all features work as specified. Only minor improvements noted above.
